source_name: Code4rena Noya Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Invalid handling of holding positions in DolomiteConnector::transferBetweenAccounts
summary: In Dolomite protocol, transferBetweenAccounts transfers the collateral from 1 account to another under the same owner. So the owner of both accounts still owns these funds regardless of the account they’re deposited in. In DolomiteConnector, when calling transferBetweenAccounts the protocol isn’t updating the holding position of either account.
root_cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in Invalid handling of holding positions in DolomiteConnector::transferBetweenAccounts, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
broken_invariants: collateral management valuation must remain correct under the root cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in Invalid handling of holding positions in DolomiteConnector::transferBetweenAccounts, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
privileged, owner, and license checks must be enforced on every value-moving path, not only on the nominal entry point
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: In Dolomite protocol, transferBetweenAccounts transfers the collateral from 1 account to another under the same owner. So the owner of both accounts still owns these funds regardless of the account they’re deposited in. In DolomiteConnector, when calling transferBetweenAccounts the protocol isn’t updating the holding position of either account.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Add the following at the bottom of DolomiteConnector::transferBetweenAccounts: (uint256[] memory markets0,,,) = dolomiteMargin.getAccountBalances(Info(address(this), 0)); registry.updateHoldingPosition( vaultId, registry.calculatePositionId(address(this), DOL_POSITION_ID, ""), abi.encode(0), "", markets0.length == 0 ); (uint256[] memory markets1,,,) = dolomiteMargin.getAccountBalances(Info(address(this), accountId)); registry.updateHoldingPosition( vaultId, registry.calculatePositionId(address(this), DOL_POSITION_ID, ""), abi.encode(accountId), "", markets1.length == 0 ); This updates the holding positions of both accounts, by either setting or removing the position according to the open markets of each.
